raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California Governor Gavin Newsom has signed AB 1979: The Doxing Recourse Victims Act into law.",
    "The Doxing Recourse Victims Act will allow victims to pursue civil action and receive restitution for harm endured due to doxing.",
    "Doxing involves publicly revealing private information about an individual without their consent.",
    "Online harassment, including doxing, can lead to identity theft, harassment, stalking, physical harm, and even death.",
    "An estimated 11 million Americans report they have been the victim of doxing, constituting around 4% of the American population.",
    "About half of doxing victims reported that their home addresses or emails were made public.",
    "One in four doxing victims said that photos or videos of them were distributed online without their consent.",
    "Current California law provides few resources for doxing victims to hold offenders accountable or recover damages.",
    "Victims will be able to pursue damages not exceeding $30,000 for pain and suffering under the new legislation."
  ]
}
```
